commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 2026-07-20

The liquidity hierarchy divides sharply into three tiers. Nasdaq 100 and NYSE dominate, combining minimal price impact (0.0047% and 0.0056% per $1M traded respectively) with substantial comparable volume ($264.4M and $191.3M). Tokyo Stock Exchange ranks third with moderate ILLIQ of 0.0351% per $1M but stands out for elevated turnover ratio of 3.0758%, suggesting concentrated retail participation relative to market cap. The middle tier—Euronext, Hong Kong, and Korea Exchange—cluster around 0.03–0.14% ILLIQ per $1M, though Korea Exchange's 1.4667% AR spread signals significantly wider execution costs despite modest $17.8M comparable volume. The laggards (SIX Swiss, Xetra, London Stock Exchange) show deteriorating price impact ranging from 0.0443% to 0.0754% per $1M and notably low comparable volumes under $9M. A striking divergence appears at SIX Swiss Exchange: its 0.5540% AR spread ranks among the tightest, yet ILLIQ of 0.0443% per $1M and minimal $3.2M turnover suggest limited depth beyond tight nominal spreads—a critical distinction for large institutional traders.
